content: Scenario 18 (warren-82ea) is the only acceptance scenario that boots its own warren+burrow topology inside run(). It calls bootInProcMulti (scripts/acceptance/lib/inproc.ts) to spawn N burrows with auth-on (no --no-auth, BURROW_API_TOKEN per worker) plus warren via WARREN_CONFIG_FILE pointing at a generated warren.toml with [[workers]] rows. Distinct from bootInProc to keep the single-burrow scenarios 01-17 untouched.

stub-shell agent in the acceptance harness (scripts/acceptance/lib/stub-agent/agent.sh) emits raw-text stdout; burrow's raw-text parser never produces a state_change with type=result|agent_end, so warren's detectRuntimeTerminal (src/runs/stream.ts) never fires and the run row stays 'running' forever. Scenarios that need a naturally-terminating run must use the 'pi' agent (which emits agent_end via pi-stub-agent.sh) or explicitly POST /runs/:id/cancel. Scenario 18 uses 'pi' for runs whose succeeded state matters (affinity warm-up, post-drain failover) and 'stub-shell' with [sleep_ms=8000] + cancel for runs that must stay in-flight.

Two-project fixtures (scenario 18): warren.fixtures.ts only builds one sample project. To get a second project from the same fixture (needed for affinity-vs-other-project assertions), the scenario appends a second [url "<sampleProjectPath>"] / insteadOf rewrite to a per-scenario git-config file before bootInProcMulti, then POST /projects with a distinct fake gitUrl (e.g. https://github.com/warren-acceptance/sample-b.git). warren keys projects by gitUrl so the two URLs yield independent project rows backed by the same on-disk source repo.

name: ScenarioSkipped: env-gated scenario skip protocol
description: Acceptance scenarios can throw ScenarioSkipped (or call skipScenario(reason)) from scripts/acceptance/lib/assert.ts when a precondition isn't met (e.g. scenario 19 / warren-on-postgres requires WARREN_TEST_PG_URL). runScenarios catches it and records the scenario with status='skipped' + error=<reason>; the harness exit code stays 0 (skip is not failure). Used by scenario 19 so the default 'bun run scripts/acceptance/run.ts' invocation stays green on a stock dev box while CI matrix jobs that opt in light up the gated scenario. Distinct from the mode='in-proc' / mode='container' skip mechanism — that's per-scenario static metadata, ScenarioSkipped is per-invocation runtime detection. Future env-gated scenarios (real-LLM scenarios behind --real today) should adopt the same shape rather than throwing AcceptanceError.

title: Per-scenario fresh CREATE DATABASE (not search_path schema) for scenario 19 pg isolation
rationale:
Two isolation shapes were on the table for scripts/acceptance/scenarios/19-warren-on-postgres.ts. (1) Per-scenario schema + search_path option, mirroring withDb() in src/db/testing.ts (mx-73932c). (2) Per-scenario CREATE DATABASE on a maintenance connection. Picked option 2.

Why not search_path schema: withDb() works because the test process opens drizzle itself and threads migrationsSchema through migratePg, so drizzle's __drizzle_migrations bookkeeping lives inside the per-test schema and is reaped by DROP SCHEMA CASCADE. Scenario 19 spawns warren as a subprocess via bootInProc; warren's openDatabase (src/db/client.ts) does NOT thread migrationsSchema (production doesn't need it). If scenario 19 isolated by search_path only, drizzle would write its journal to the default 'drizzle' schema and skip migrations on the next scenario run (journal hashes match) — yielding empty per-scenario schemas and broken warren boot. Widening warren's openDatabase surface for a test concern is also a smell.

Why CREATE DATABASE: a brand-new database has its own pristine drizzle schema. Warren's migrations run cleanly; bookkeeping lives in the per-scenario DB and is reaped along with it. Teardown uses DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS … WITH (FORCE) (pg ≥13) to evict any lingering pool clients from warren's pg.Pool that didn't disconnect on handle.stop().

Trade-off: the WARREN_TEST_PG_URL role must hold CREATEDB privilege. That's stricter than CREATE SCHEMA, but in practice CI matrix jobs run pg as the superuser via docker-compose and dev operators dedicate a pg instance to acceptance — both meet CREATEDB. Documented in the scenario header and on the skip message.

content: bootInProc.dbUrl override (R-13 / warren-480a) accepts any sqlite-or-pg URL and writes only WARREN_DB_URL into the spawned warren's env (omitting the default WARREN_DB_PATH). Scenarios that need a per-test pg backend pass { dbUrl: 'postgres://...?options=...' } and skip the default '$tmpRoot/data/warren.db' sqlite path. Used by scripts/acceptance/scenarios/19-warren-on-postgres.ts; the loader's path↔url conflict warning (warren-e2ea) doesn't fire because WARREN_DB_PATH is not exported alongside.

description: The repo layer is still sqlite-only (mx-994fa5: every repo uses bun-sqlite's synchronous .run() / .get() / .all()), and src/server/main.ts:336-344 (createReposForDialect) explicitly throws when db.dialect !== 'sqlite'. Plan pl-f17e bundled the pg widening implicitly into step 7 (warren-480a, this seed), but warren-480a's seed title is just the acceptance scenario — the repo widening is a much larger refactor that was never broken out as its own step. Result: scenario 19 lands as a skip-gated harness contract; flipping the gate to actually run end-to-end on pg is blocked.
resolution: Wrote the scenario + harness machinery (SkipScenarioError, bootInProc.dbUrl wiring already in place from prior step). Filed warren-0f34 for the widening + gate lift. Scenario 19 flips from skipped → passed when warren-0f34 lands and WARREN_TEST_PG_URL is set.

name: preview-scenario-own-stack-per-variant
description: Acceptance scenario 20 boots a separate warren+burrow stack per variant (A=happy path, B=idle-TTL eviction) so each variant can pin its own WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST / WARREN_PREVIEW_IDLE_TTL / WARREN_PREVIEW_EVICTION_TICK_MS at boot — env knobs the harness-shared stack can't expose mid-run. Mirrors scenario 19's own-stack pattern (warren-480a) and uses bootInProc with a per-variant tmpRoot under \`os.mkdtemp\`. Each variant builds its own preview-enabled source repo (cp'd from ctx.fixtures.sampleProjectPath + augmented with .warren/defaults.json) under a unique fake git URL plus an augmented git-config that extends the outer harness redirects, keeping the shared fixture untouched and other scenarios unaffected.

content: Preview acceptance scenarios skip on darwin via ScenarioSkipped(mx-1d31f0 message) because burrow's inbound-port-forwarding is Linux-only (bwrap netns + nsenter+nc relay in node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli/src/provider/local/inbound-forward.ts). Seatbelt doesn't isolate the netns on macOS, so the allocator's host port has nothing to forward to and the readiness probe times out. Skip is also gated on WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres because the port allocator + eviction worker are SQLite-only today (src/server/main.ts:160-161, 253-254); pg path is the pl-f17e follow-up.

Variant B (idle-TTL eviction) in scenario 20 deliberately makes NO proxy requests after waiting for preview_state='live' so the idle clock falls back to preview_started_at instead of preview_last_hit_at — eviction fires deterministically WARREN_PREVIEW_IDLE_TTL ms after the launcher persists the live transition. A single proxy 200 would otherwise debounce-flush preview_last_hit_at (debounce 30s by default, src/preview/proxy.ts:67) and the eviction clock would reset, making the wait nondeterministic. Variant A flips this by setting WARREN_PREVIEW_IDLE_TTL=30m so its proxy 200/401 assertions don't race the worker.

Preview-enabled fixture build in scripts/acceptance/scenarios/20-preview.ts uses python3 -m http.server as preview.command for portability — universally on PATH across linux runners and inherited into the sandbox via PASSTHROUGH_ENV_KEYS=PATH (scripts/acceptance/lib/inproc.ts:467). The fixture commits a .warren/preview-www/index.html marker containing 'warren-preview-ok' so a proxy 200 with that body in the response proves end-to-end routing (host header parse → cookie verify → upstream forward → sidecar response) instead of accepting any 200. Marker is asserted with substring match via response.bodySnippet.includes('preview-ok').

description: Skip-gated acceptance scenarios mask shipped bugs when CI doesn't set the gating env var. Scenario 19 (warren-on-postgres) skip-gates on WARREN_TEST_PG_URL via ScenarioSkipped — designed so 'bun run scripts/acceptance/run.ts' stays green on a stock machine. .github/workflows/ci.yml never sets WARREN_TEST_PG_URL and only runs 'bun test' (unit) — acceptance scenarios are run separately. Net: scenario 19 has never actually executed in CI, allowing the half-shipped R-13 (warren-2680) to merge unchecked. Lesson: skip-gated scenarios need explicit opt-in CI coverage, not just 'lights up when env is set' — without the matrix wire-up, the gate is silent.
resolution: warren-fe03 plans the fix: add a postgres:16 service container to a CI matrix job, set WARREN_TEST_PG_URL on that job, run scenario 19 there. Folded into plan pl-f1be step 6. Going forward: any new skip-gated scenario should be paired with a CI matrix entry as part of the same PR — file an issue if you find a skip gate without a corresponding CI opt-in.

content: Scenario 19 (warren-on-postgres, warren-d4c6) verified green against real postgres:16 after R-13 adapter migration (pl-f1be steps 1-7). Three consecutive in-proc runs ~3.7-3.8s each with WARREN_TEST_PG_URL=postgres://warren:warren@127.0.0.1:54319/warren_test against an ephemeral docker postgres:16 container. The scenario CREATEs a per-run database (suffix warren_acc19_<hex>), boots warren+burrow there, validates /readyz db_reachable.message='dialect=postgres', spawns a stub run, kills warren mid-stream, restarts, and asserts events resume from MAX(burrow_event_seq)+1 with no seq gaps. Closes pl-f1be acceptance #3.

Path-mode preview acceptance scenarios (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/20-preview-path.ts, warren-7b3c) boot warren with WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE=path and deliberately leave WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST unset — that's the zero-domain operator assertion the path-mode plan exists to make. Path-mode proxy preamble + login handler both wire up when only mode=path is set (no host required), per src/server/main.ts:286,331.

Asserting the path-mode HTML <base> rewrite (warren-ab3a) from an acceptance scenario requires the upstream fixture to serve a real HTML document with a <head> tag — bare-text files served as text/html (e.g. scenario 20's 'warren-preview-ok\n' index) skip injection because injectBaseHref returns null on documents with no parseable <head> within HTML_HEAD_LOOKAHEAD_BYTES. scripts/acceptance/scenarios/20-preview-path.ts (warren-7b3c) writes a real <html><head>…</head><body>…</body></html> document so the assertion lands.

Scenario 21 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/21-claude-code-cost-smoke.ts, warren-87f9) is the claude-code parallel of scenario 16's pi cost smoke. It dispatches POST /runs agent=claude-code against a stub runtime registered in burrow-with-stub.ts that OVERRIDES burrow's built-in claude-code runtime — burrow's AgentRegistry uses Map.set so client.agents.register(buildClaudeAcceptanceRuntime()) with id='claude-code' cleanly replaces the real-CLI-invoking built-in. Stub script tools/claude-code-stub-agent.sh emits stream-json with a fixed result envelope (cost 0.000421, 1200 input, 400 output, 5000 cache_read, 200 cache_create); scenario asserts all five run columns are non-null + positive after terminal.

description: Preview acceptance scenarios (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/20-preview*.ts) exclusively use 'python3 -m http.server' as the sidecar (per mx-87d34c). The pi-compat node-shim symlink (Dockerfile:89) only manifests when the sidecar's dev command resolves through Node's bin shebangs — so python http server runs cleanly while every real Node JS framework (next/vite/remix) hits Bun's built-in module gaps. 2026-05-15 dogfood (warren-a82b) discovered this only because run_jegavdzfbrpc on jayminwest.com used 'pnpm dev'. CI was green throughout. The acceptance coverage shape (probe a port, not exercise the runtime) was the gap.
resolution: Add a Node-runtime preview scenario alongside 20-preview*.ts that boots a minimal 'next dev' or 'vite dev' inside the burrow and asserts 2xx on /. Keep the python http scenario for the proxy/HTML-rewrite paths (it's faster). Run both on CI. Optionally: a scenario per package manager (npm/pnpm/bun) to catch installer-shape regressions.

content: Scenario 22 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/22-seeds-extensions-roundtrip.ts, warren-65ef) is the manual-dispatch half of the R-01 seeds-extensions producer contract: POST /runs with seedId stamps {role,trigger:'manual',lastRunId,lastRunAt} onto the seed via sd update --extensions; a bogus seedId surfaces a seeds_extension_write_failed system event without rolling the run back. Scenario 15 covers the cron-tick half (trigger:'scheduled' + scheduledFor clear + lastScheduledRun). Both share the bootstrap-config.yaml-if-missing posture so they can run against the harness's shared sample project clone without contaminating sibling scenarios; both reset the source repo's .seeds/issues.jsonl at teardown for idempotent re-runs.

Scenario 23 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/23-canopy-project-tier.ts, warren-b2f9) covers R-03 / pl-fef5 project-tier roundtrip end-to-end. Pattern: bootstrap project-tier .canopy/ in the sample-source repo via 'cn init' + 'cn create' + 'cn sync' (mirrors the canopy-source fixture's pattern), commit, then POST /projects/:id/refresh so the warren clone fetches it. Re-uses the existing stub-shell agent name at project tier so listing/spawn override behavior is observable against the library tier scenario 02 registers. Acceptance #7 of pl-fef5 originally called for slot 22, but 22 was claimed by seeds-extensions-roundtrip; scenario lands at 23.

Scenario 24 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/24-preview-node-runtime.ts, warren-0c8e) is the post-warren-a82b regression check for the bun-shim → real-Node Dockerfile change: preview command is 'node ./.warren/preview-server.js' and the script exits 1 when process.versions.bun is defined, so if /usr/local/bin/node ever gets re-symlinked at the bun-shim the readiness probe deadlines out. Skips if 'node' is not on host PATH (in-proc mode forwards host PATH into the sandbox via PASSTHROUGH_ENV_KEYS); deployed container always has Node 22 from NodeSource (Dockerfile:64-65). Mirrors scenario 20's subdomain-mode boot pattern + cookie-gated proxy GET; assertion is the body marker 'warren-preview-node-ok'.

Scenario 25 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/25-plot-roundtrip.ts, warren-4e06) is the Plot integration end-to-end roundtrip for pl-2047. Builds its own Plot-enabled fixture project under ctx.tmp/scenario-25-fixture (mirrors scenario 18's two-project pattern: appends an insteadOf line to the shared ctx.tmp/git-config so warren clones via the fake gitUrl). Asserts: (a) hasPlot=true after clone (warren-4e20), (b) host-side run_dispatched lands on the project's .plot/pl-<id>.events.jsonl post-spawn (warren-e848), (c) no-plot dispatch is byte-identical (no plot.* events, plotId null on every NDJSON envelope, no stub-agent PLOT_ID echo). Env-reaches-sandbox + sandbox plot append + reap-time mirror assertions are soft-skipped behind a logger.warn block — they depend on burrow-cli@0.3.1 parsing body.env (filed warren-a346).

name: acceptance-soft-skip-upstream-blocked
description: Soft-skip pattern for upstream-dependency-blocked assertions: when an end-to-end acceptance assertion is blocked on a known upstream gap (e.g. warren-a346 — burrow's POST /burrows doesn't yet parse body.env so warren-e26f's PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR don't reach the sandbox), guard the dependent assertions with an 'envReached'-style precondition check that demotes them to ctx.logger.warn rather than throw. Tag both the JSDoc header and the in-body comment with a SOFT_SKIP_REASON marker pointing to the follow-up seed id, and structure the conditional so that when the upstream lands the assertions auto-promote to hard requirements (no rewrite needed). Scenario 25 (warren-4e06) is the prototype.

content: Scenario 26 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/26-plan-run-roundtrip.ts, warren-ae00) is the plan-run roundtrip. Three children dispatched serially via the coordinator against an in-proc warren+burrow stack. Uses agent=claude-code (NOT stub-shell — raw-text outputFormat never emits a runtime-terminal envelope so detectRuntimeTerminal never fires, leaving warren runs stuck in 'running' forever; that's why scenarios 04/09/10 cancel mid-run). WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE=merged short-circuits GH REST calls inside src/runs/pr.ts (openPullRequest + checkPullRequestMerged) and loadAutoOpenPrConfigFromEnv synthesizes a stub token so reap's empty-token gate doesn't skip pr_open. WARREN_STUB_NO_COMMIT_SEEDS is a comma-list (forwarded via burrow.toml [env].optional in fixtures.ts) of seed ids whose closeseed dispatch produces no commits — drives the coordinator's trivial-merge branch (commitsAhead=0 + reap.empty_push → child merged without GH polling).

Scenario 27 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/27-plan-run-plot-roundtrip.ts, warren-97a3 / pl-7937 step 8) is the PlanRun + Plot composition roundtrip — boots a per-scenario warren stack (in-proc only, mirrors scenario 26) against a fixture committing both .seeds/ (two plans, four seeds — pl-acc-27-plot has SEED_A/B/C with SEED_C in WARREN_STUB_NO_COMMIT_SEEDS for the trivial-merge branch; pl-acc-27-base has SEED_D for the no-plot baseline) and .plot/ (one Plot pre-transitioned drafting → ready → active by the fixture so the auto-done has an active plot to terminate). Asserts the full warren-c900/b89f/b290 surface end-to-end: plan_run.plotId echoed in POST response, plan_run_dispatched landed on the Plot, three children all merged with run.plotId set, per-child run_dispatched accumulated for every child including the trivial-merge, plot.json status flipped to 'done', status_changed → done event by user:operator landed, and plan_run.plot_auto_done on the plan-run event stream. SOFT_SKIP for the per-child PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR sandbox env (warren-a346, shared with scenario 25). Byte-identical baseline: a SECOND POST without plot_id (using pl-acc-27-base/SEED_D so children remain open) returns plotId=null and emits zero new Plot events for its own IDs.

description: Naive 'read .plot/<id>.events.jsonl once after plan completes' assertions miss per-child run_dispatched and the host-side plan_run_dispatched event — refreshProject (src/projects/refresh.ts:139, git reset --hard origin/<ref>) fires before each spawnRun and discards uncommitted appender writes between children, so the on-disk events.jsonl after plan-success only contains the LAST surviving writes (the final child's run_dispatched + the auto-done status_changed). Similarly, plot.json status='done' reverts to 'active' after any post-auto-done refresh.
resolution: Tail the events.jsonl every ~100ms throughout plan execution with a setInterval poller that accumulates unique lines into a Set, started BEFORE the POST so plan_run_dispatched is captured even if the coordinator's first tick races the first poll. After waitForPlanState succeeds, wait ~1.5s (POST_PLAN_TAIL_FLUSH_MS) and tickOnce() once more so the auto-done's status_changed lands in the Set before parsing. See startPlotEventsTail() in scripts/acceptance/scenarios/27-plan-run-plot-roundtrip.ts. Underlying production issue tracked at warren-6f25.

content: scripts/acceptance/lib/stub-agent/claude-code-agent.sh emits a 'claude-stub: PLOT_ID=<id> PLOT_ACTOR=<actor>' text assistant envelope (jsonl-claude format) when PLOT_ID is present in env — added for scenario 27 (warren-97a3) to give the per-child PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR sandbox-injection assertion an agent-side hook. Gated on PLOT_ID so plan-run scenarios that don't bind a Plot (26, 17) are unaffected. Mirrors the existing PLOT_ID echo on stub-shell agent.sh (warren-4e06). Currently soft-skipped pending warren-a346 (burrow doesn't forward body.env into the sandbox).

Scenario 29 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/29-plot-detail-roundtrip.ts, warren-c40b / pl-9d6a step 18) is the Plot detail roundtrip — in-proc per-scenario stack mirroring scenario 27's WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE=merged + WARREN_STUB_NO_COMMIT_SEEDS + WARREN_PLAN_RUN_TICK_MS knobs. Fixture commits .seeds/ (two children + one two-child plan pl-acc-29ab) AND .plot/ (one Plot pre-transitioned drafting→ready→active, two pre-attached refs, one agent-authored question_posed). Asserts the full envelope shape on GET /plots/:id, intent_edited round-trip, three actually-illegal transitions from active (drafting/ready/active — NOT done; done is legal per STATUS_TRANSITIONS, the seed text 'active→done requires plan completion' doesn't match the implementation and is documented inline), attach/detach roundtrip, question answer + already-answered 409, then Run-plan via POST /plan-runs with plot_id reusing scenario 27's Plot-events-file tail to verify plan_run_dispatched + per-child run_dispatched + auto-done. mergePlot mirror SOFT_SKIP shared with scenarios 25/27 (warren-a346 pending).

Plot fixture sd_plan attachment convention (warren-5d94 / src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx isSdPlanAttachment): @os-eco/plot-cli v0.3 has no first-class sd_plan AttachmentType. Acceptance scenarios that need an sd_plan attachment use kind='seeds_issue' with a ref matching /^pl-/i (typically the .seeds/ plan id). The seeds_issue ref pattern at the warren handler edge (ATTACHMENT_REF_PATTERNS in src/server/handlers.ts) requires /^[a-z0-9_-]+-[a-f0-9]{4}$/, so the plan id's last 4 chars must be hex (e.g. pl-acc-29ab works; pl-acc-29-plot does NOT). Scenario 29 picks plan ids accordingly.

Plot UI Phase 3 acceptance is split across scenarios 28 + 29 (warren-5b8a + warren-c40b): 28 covers /plots list+create across mixed hasPlot/non-hasPlot projects with the byte-identical zero-leakage /runs snapshot on the non-hasPlot project; 29 covers the full PlotDetail roundtrip — intent edits, status-transition rejections per SPEC §6.5, attachment add/remove, already-answered question rejection, and Run-plan → POST /plan-runs → Plot auto-done composition that reuses §11.P.Plot wiring (mx-92e6b3 + mx-90f430). Both follow the mx-af2627 / mx-15e4da in-proc warren-stack pattern with idempotent teardown. The Plot UI release gate is scenarios 25 + 27 + 28 + 29 all green, not just the new ones — composition with Phase 1 (25) and Phase 2 (27) must still hold.

Scenario 25 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/25-plot-roundtrip.ts, warren-49d7 / pl-95dd step 2) PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR/plot-append/plot.decision_made mirror assertions are now hard-required (pre-warren-a346 they were soft-skipped behind an envReached conditional that wrapped them under ctx.logger.warn). The flip is consumer-side only — burrow-59cd shipped + burrow-cli pin bumped (warren-0a6b) makes burrow parse body.env into BurrowUpInput.envOverrides, so PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR reach the sandbox. Each missing artifact now throws AcceptanceError with a tailored message; the ctx.logger.warn branch and the envReached gate are gone. Any future regression in the warren→burrow env-forwarding contract fails CI loudly here.

description: warren-73ee (Run scenario 25 end-to-end against a live stack) is transitively blocked on warren-0a6b (the burrow-cli double-pin bump), which is itself blocked on burrow-59cd shipping to npm. Re-verified 2026-05-18: latest published @os-eco/burrow-cli is 0.3.1, which is already what package.json/bun.lock/Dockerfile pin, and node_modules/@os-eco/burrow-cli/src/server/handlers.ts createBurrow still drops body.env. Running scenario 25 now would deterministically fail the newly hard-required (warren-49d7) Plot env-reach assertions — PLOT_ID is silently dropped before the sandbox sees it, so the stub agent's 'claude-stub: PLOT_ID=<id>' echo never carries a value, the plot.decision_made mirror never fires, and the workspace .plot/<id>.events.jsonl gets no agent append. No useful 'live stack' signal can be produced without first landing warren-0a6b.
resolution: Do not attempt to execute warren-73ee until warren-0a6b closes. The sequencing is strict: (1) burrow publishes >0.3.1 with the body.env parse fix; (2) warren-0a6b bumps all three pins and verifies env reaches sandbox; (3) only then run 'bun run scripts/acceptance/run.ts --only 25-plot-roundtrip' against an in-proc warren+burrow stack (scenario 25 is in-proc only — see file header). If that passes, proceed to warren-0eb0 (CHANGELOG + patch bump).

content: Scenario 27 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/27-plan-run-plot-roundtrip.ts) reads .plot/<id>.events.jsonl directly from disk post-completion via readPlotEventLines() rather than tailing the file every 100ms — the prior tail workaround existed because refreshProjectClone's git reset --hard wiped uncommitted Plot appends between children, but warren-fdd2 (pl-d4d6 step 1) added snapshot+restore of .plot/ across the reset so every host-side append now survives. Removed in warren-aa63 / pl-d4d6 step 3. When on-disk warren-managed state needs to round-trip across spawnRun refreshes, rely on refreshProjectClone's .plot/ snapshot for Plot dirs; for non-Plot dirs commit-through-reap is still the only durable channel (shape (a), filed as warren-36b5).

name: pi-stub-env-passthrough-probe
description: Acceptance proof for runtime-level env passthrough (burrow-6f3f / warren-fe96): (1) scripts/acceptance/lib/burrow-with-stub.ts swaps buildPiAcceptanceRuntime.envPassthrough to the real burrow piEnvPassthrough so the dispatcher's applyRuntimeEnvPassthrough runs against the harness's stub runtime, (2) scripts/acceptance/lib/stub-agent/pi-agent.sh emits {type:'env_keys_visible',keys:[...]} at startup listing which provider keys it observed in its env, (3) scenarios then set process.env.<PROVIDER>_API_KEY before dispatching with providerOverride=<provider>, parse the (state_change-collapsed) env_keys_visible event off /runs/:id/events, and assert the expected key is in the list. Pattern is reusable for future runtime-level env passthrough changes (e.g. claude-code/sapling/codex adding similar conditionality).

name: scenario-31-plot-plan-run-synthesis
description: Scenario 31 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/31-plot-plan-run-synthesis.ts, warren-af97 / pl-f404 step 5 / SPEC §11.Q) is the plot-plan-run synthesis roundtrip. Composes scenarios 25+27+29 against one .plot/+.seeds/-enabled fixture: POST /plot-plan-runs → synthesizer mints throwaway parent seed + plan via existing_seed adoption (seeds-cli 0.4.7), 3 open seeds_issue attachments become children (closed + sd_plan-shaped refs filtered at handler edge), walks to terminal succeeded via WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE=merged + WARREN_STUB_NO_COMMIT_SEEDS=SEED_C for trivial-merge, auto-dones the Plot, re-dispatch mints a new synthesized plan_id. Covers SPEC §11.Q acceptance 1-4, 5 (plot_id_invalid + plot_id_not_found + project_lacks_plot + no_dispatchable_seeds typed 4xx), and 6 (no-clobber re-dispatch). Slot 30 was already claimed by 30-pi-multi-provider-env.ts (warren-fe96) when this seed landed, so the filename slot moves to 31 (SPEC §11.Q text updated accordingly).

content: Scenario 29 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/29-plot-detail-roundtrip.ts) event_log assertion uses 'attachment_added' to match @os-eco/plot-cli 0.3.0 emit names; older 'attachment_attached' name is wrong. Other code paths (src/ui types, src/plots, src/plot-client, src/server/handlers.plots.test.ts) already used 'attachment_added' — scenario 29 was the lone holdout.

description: Scenario 27 (warren-9e2c) expected the no-agent-commit child to keep prUrl=null via the trivial-merge branch, but warren-343a's reap.stagePlotForCommit lands a 'chore(warren): plot state' commit on every plot-bound run carrying host-side .plot/ appender writes (per-child run_dispatched, plan_run_dispatched, status_changed) back to origin. commitsAhead is therefore ≥ 1 even when the agent makes no commit, reap auto-opens a PR, and the trivial-merge contract only holds for plot-LESS projects (scenario 26).
resolution: Update scenario 27 to assert that the no-agent-commit SEED_C run DOES open a PR (prUrl non-null) rather than stays null. Rename trivialChild/trivialRun → noAgentCommitChild/noAgentCommitRun and refresh the doc comments so the test intent matches the post-warren-343a contract. Scenario 26 still covers the plot-less trivial-merge path.

content: Scenario 25 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/25-plot-roundtrip.ts, warren-73ee / pl-95dd step 3) verified green end-to-end against a live in-proc warren+burrow stack with @os-eco/burrow-cli@0.3.3 (the burrow-59cd release). All four previously soft-skipped Plot assertions — PLOT_ID echo, PLOT_ACTOR echo, agent plot-append OK, reap mirror of plot.decision_made — run unconditionally and pass. Sibling scenario 27 (PlanRun + Plot) also green so the body.env parse fix did not regress the parallel plan-run path. Run command: 'bun run scripts/acceptance/run.ts --only 25'. Typical wall time ~4.6s. macOS works (in-proc only, modes: ['in-proc']).

name: Decomposing sequential acceptance scenarios into scenarios/lib siblings
description: When an acceptance scenario file grows beyond the 500-line budget, it should be decomposed by extracting shared setup, assertion, and request-builder helpers into modular sibling files under scripts/acceptance/scenarios/lib/. This keeps the scenario orchestrator thin, legible, and easy to maintain while enabling reusability.

content: Decomposed scripts/acceptance/scenarios/31-plot-plan-run-synthesis.ts (950 lines) by extracting shared synthesis fixtures, build helpers, and git config redirect writers into scripts/acceptance/scenarios/lib/fixture-31.ts; leveraged existing poll-helpers and event-helpers to reduce the orchestrator scenario file under 500 lines.

Decomposed scripts/acceptance/scenarios/27-plan-run-plot-roundtrip.ts (856 lines) into scenarios/lib/fixture-27.ts and imported shared poll/event helpers, lowering file-size budget below 500-line ceiling.

Scenario 33 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/33-leveret-conversation-loop.ts, warren-9f47) is the leveret conversation-loop acceptance: conversation create (mode='conversation' run hidden from the Runs list, reachable by id), many operator turns (transcript persists, conversation survives, status stays active), send-off (plotSync PR via WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE=merged + conversation close + anchoring run finalizes succeeded), merge-poller planner auto-dispatch (WARREN_MERGE_POLLER_ENABLED=1 tick=500ms, planner_agent pinned to stub-shell on send-off so the burrow can run it), and re-plan as a NEW conversation on the SAME Plot. Keeps the anchoring run alive across turns+send-off via a [sleep_ms=120000] prompt knob. Reuses scenario 32's buildFixture/waitForRunState helpers. SOFT_SKIPs (mx-384467): leveret propose_intent->intent_edited (warren-ce65, intent driven via POST /plots/:id/intent instead), idle-finalize (warren-005d), re-wake (warren-6ccf), operator-gated plan-run dispatch (warren-6e45/warren-d622).

Scenario 34 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/34-warden-conversation-acceptance.ts, warren-6022 / pl-da54 step 4) is the audit warden conversation acceptance: seeds the standing warden conversation (title='Audit Warden', mode='conversation', bound to meta-Plot), posts three real-shaped auditor findings (gatewatch/ratchetwatch/tastewatch format) over POST /conversations/:id/messages, fires the weekly warden-digest synthesis message, verifies the 5-message transcript, then exercises the full send-off → merge-poller → planner auto-dispatch chain (WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE=merged + WARREN_MERGE_POLLER_ENABLED=1 tick=500ms, planner_agent=stub-shell). Asserts: conversation resolvable by title filter on GET /conversations?status=active, all auditor findings accepted as 202, closed conversation rejects further messages, plannerRunId stamped after merge-poller fires, planner run terminates (plan proposed through chain with no new dispatch path). SOFT_SKIP (warren-ce65 pending): intent edit driven via POST /plots/:id/intent as stand-in. Registers at slot 34 in scripts/acceptance/run.ts. Mirrors scenario 33's in-proc boot knobs.

Scenario 33 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/33-leveret-conversation-loop.ts, warren-1198 / pl-0008 step 12) was reframed from 'Leveret conversation loop' to the Workspace flow walk: list (GET /plots ⋈ GET /conversations) → Shape (conversation create + turns + re-wake + intent) → send-off → Plan (merge-poller planner auto-dispatch) → Run. The former Phase-D SOFT_SKIP for operator-gated plan-run dispatch is now a real Run leg in scripts/acceptance/scenarios/lib/scenario-33.ts (driveWorkspaceRunLeg): it walks the Plot drafting→ready→active via POST /plots/:id/status, dispatches POST /plan-runs (agent claude-code, plotId-bound) mirroring the Plan tab DispatchPlanButton, asserts every child reaches merged and the Plot auto-transitions to done (SPEC §11.P). The fixture (built by the scenario-32 helper) is extended host-side via extendFixtureWithPlan with a plan + two open child seeds and re-committed before clone. Boot env adds WARREN_PLAN_RUN_TICK_MS=1000 alongside WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE=merged. Helper file kept under 500 lines by hoisting wire-shape interfaces + waitForPlannerDispatch into lib/scenario-33.ts.

title: CI-fixer acceptance scenario is live-GitHub env-gated, not stubbed
rationale: The poller calls real api.github.com check-runs and only acts on real Actions verdicts; the harness's local-git/insteadOf fixtures cannot fake that, so a faithful scenario must hit live GitHub. Skip-gating (like scenario 19) keeps the default suite green while a secret-wired CI job lights it up.

content: Scenario 36 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/36-ready-to-dispatch-plans.ts, warren-f16c / pl-3fc4 step 8) is the ready-to-dispatch-plans roundtrip. In-proc twin of scenario 26: builds a per-scenario .seeds/ fixture with one approved plan (pl-acc-36) carrying a single open child, asserts GET /projects/:id/ready-plans surfaces it with openChildCount=1, dispatches via POST /plan-runs, then asserts the plan drops out of ready-plans (dedup via PlanRunsRepo.listDispatchedPlanIds). Uses WARREN_GH_FETCH_OVERRIDE=merged + claude-code stub agent so the dispatched child finalizes.
